latest.title: Exercise of Warrants & Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 11:30
RNS Number : 8091D
Great Western Mining Corp. plc
11 May 2026
GREAT WESTERN MINING CORPORATION PLC
("Great Western", "GWM" or the "Company")
EXERCISE OF WARRANTS AND ISSUE OF EQUITY
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC (AIM: GWMO & ESM: 8GW) announces that it has received a notice of exercise of warrants over 10,944,875 new ordinary shares of €0.0001 each in the share capital of the Company at a price of 1.30 pence per share ("the Warrant Shares"), which were granted in conjunction with the Placing announced on 10 June 2025. The Company has approved the issue of the Warrant Shares with gross proceeds amounting to £142,283.38.
Settlement and Dealing
Application will be made to the AIM Market of the London Stock Exchange ("AIM") and Euronext Growth for a total of 10,944,875 ordinary shares, which will rank pari passu with the Company's existing issued Ordinary Shares, to be admitted to trading. Dealings on AIM and Euronext Growth are expected to commence on or around 15 May 2026 ("Admission").
Total Voting Rights
In conformity with Regulation 20 of the Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007, Great Western advises that the total number of ordinary shares of €0.0001 each in issue (with voting rights) will be 425,939,981 following the issue of the Warrant Shares. The Company does not hold any ordinary shares in treasury.
This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in Great Western under the Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007 and the Transparency Rules.
For further information visit

latest.title: Metallurgical Test Work Underway: Tungsten Project
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3220D
Great Western Mining Corp. plc
07 May 2026
GREAT WESTERN MINING CORPORATION PLC
("Great Western", "GWM" or the "Company")
Metallurgical Test Work Underway at Tungsten Project
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC (AIM: GWMO & ESM: 8GW), the exploration and development company focused on mineral projects in Nevada, USA, is pleased to announce that it is undertaking proof-of-concept flotation test work on material from its Defender - Pine Crow Tungsten Project ("Defender - Pine Crow" or the "Project") in Mineral County, Nevada.
HIGHLIGHTS
·
Eriez Global of Erie, Pennsylvania appointed to conduct flotation test work on a 750 kg bulk sample from Defender - Pine Crow.
·
Programme to target scheelite mineralisation in skarn host rocks, applying a proven flowsheet to produce a high-grade tungsten ore concentrate with high recovery.
·
Test work to validate processing potential and advance the Project as a US-based tungsten opportunity
·
Assay results, expected in Q3 2026, to contribute towards maiden Mineral Resource Estimation ("MRE") targeted for Q4 2026.
Great Western CEO, Ed Loye, commented:
"Engaging Eriez to undertake this test work represents an important step forward in validating the metallurgical characteristics of the mineralisation at Defender - Pine Crow. By applying an established flowsheet to a representative bulk sample, we aim to confirm the suitability of conventional flotation techniques for scheelite recovery.
"We are conducting this work alongside several other activities at the Project, including geophysics, mapping and trenching, all ahead of the fully funded drill campaign scheduled to commence in July 2026. This highlights the team's confidence in the near-term potential for Defender - Pine Crow and our eagerness to expedite exploration efforts and advance towards a maiden MRE this year."
DETAILS
Metallurgical Test Work Programme
The Company has dispatched a 750 kg bulk sample of run-of-mine material from the Defender - Pine Crow opencut mines to Eriez in Erie, Pennsylvania. The sample is representative of scheelite-bearing skarn mineralisation observed at surface in previously active open-pit mines abandoned after the cessation of WWII.
Eriez will undertake proof-of-concept flotation test work using a pre-existing flowsheet applicable to this style of tungsten mineralisation. The programme is designed to assess recovery characteristics, concentrate grades and overall processing response of the material.
The work will be carried out at Eriez's Pennsylvania laboratory, a well-established facility known for metallurgical process development and simulation across a range of mineral systems.
Results from this test work will contribute to the Company's understanding of the processing pathways available for Defender - Pine Crow and will inform future metallurgical and processing workstreams.
Background
The Defender and Pine Crow tungsten skarns are located approximately 3 km northeast of and on trend with the Company's M2 skarn copper resource in Mineral County, Nevada. Historic production of tungsten from these skarns dates back to a short period during the Second World War.
Following earlier positive field mapping and grab sampling, in December 2025 the Company undertook a machine-cut channel sampling programme at Defender. Two channels returned significant tungsten mineralisation, including 6 m at 0.17% WO₃, including 1 m at 0.43% WO₃, and 16 m at 0.30% WO₃, including 2 m at 0.66% WO₃ (see RNS dated 29 January 2026). Importantly, elements that could represent potential processing penalties were recorded at very low concentrations, and silver mineralisation was also intersected within Channel B.
The Company launched a fully funded expanded exploration programme at Defender - Pine Crow during Q1 2026 to accelerate progress towards defining a maiden MRE and test its scale, continuity and geometry of the mineralised system. This work also includes testing the potential geological relationship of Defender - Pine Crow to the broader mineralised trend extending towards M2, located more than 3 km to the southwest.
Further announcements will be made in due course.
**ENDS**
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC
Brian Hall, Chairman
Ed Loye, CEO
c/o St Brides
greatwesternmining@stbridespartners.co.uk
Max Williams, Finance Director
Davy
Nominated Adviser, Euronext Growth Adviser & Joint Broker
Brian Garrahy
+353 (0)1 679 6363
Shard Capital Partners
Joint Broker
Andrew Gutmann / Erik Woolgar
+44 (0)20 7186 9008
St Brides Partners
Financial PR
Susie Geliher / Isabel de Salis
greatwesternmining@stbridespartners.co.uk
Notes
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC (AIM: GWMO / Euronext Growth: 8GW)
is an exploration and development company focused on strategic minerals across several 100%-owned claim groups in Mineral County, Nevada, a highly prospective and mining-friendly jurisdiction. The Company has an increasing strategic emphasis on tungsten as a critical mineral, while continuing to advance its Huntoon Copper Project, which hosts a JORC-compliant resource of 4.3 Mt at 0.45% Cu with further porphyry upside. It also maintains exposure to gold and silver through exploration and tailings reprocessing opportunities, alongside evaluating farm-out and joint venture options to unlock wider portfolio value.

company_30s.what_they_do: GWMO is an exploration‑focused mining company that is developing a tungsten deposit in the UK. It specialises in drilling, geological mapping and securing the necessary permits to bring the project to the surface.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tungsten is a strategic metal used in high‑strength alloys, electronics, and defence. With global supply chains shifting toward domestic sources, a new UK tungsten project can fill a critical gap and attract both industrial and governmental interest.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early exploration phase. It has not yet produced ore and is raising equity through warrants and options. Recent moves include securing a drilling contractor and extending the project area, signalling a push to move the asset closer to production.

rns_narrative: GWMO is expanding its exploration footprint by extending the project area and securing a new drilling contractor, signalling confidence in the deposit’s potential.
The company is actively raising capital—exercising warrants, issuing options, and preparing for an OTC listing—to shore up cash flow and support the next phase of drilling.
Regulatory steps such as the OTCID application and a detailed exploration plan indicate a clear path toward a public listing, which would increase liquidity and valuation.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism—few voices but a sense that a breakthrough could surprise the market.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the promise of a new tungsten deposit and the company’s strategic moves to secure funding and expand the area. Traders see the drilling contractor announcement as a potential catalyst.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of production, high cash burn, and the uncertainty of regulatory approval. They worry that the company may struggle to raise enough capital to finish drilling.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially almost silent, the latest posts show a slight uptick in optimism after the drilling contractor secured and the OTC listing application were announced.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat—no public interest yet, which means a news catalyst could create a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Positive drilling results from the newly secured contractor; a finding of mineralisation would trigger a price rally.
Approval of the OTC listing, which would provide liquidity and a formal valuation.
Any strategic partnership or additional equity round that would shore up cash reserves.
Positive commentary from independent geologists or a third‑party survey confirming the deposit’s quality.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike paired with a bullish chart pattern, the first positive drilling report, or a formal listing announcement. These signals would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early capitulation phase of a deep crash. A tight compression on the chart suggests that the next catalyst could trigger a rebound. The APEX score indicates a spring that is ready to snap.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits at a deep crash with no current public sentiment—ideal for a contrarian play. Historical deep‑crash signals have delivered large pay‑offs, and a recovery to half of the 52‑week high would roughly double the investment. A full recovery would more than triple the stake.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the drilling results are negative or the company cannot raise enough capital, it could run out of cash and face delisting or liquidation. The worst case is a further decline to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: the entire invested capital if the company fails to produce. Potential reward: 200–300% upside if the stock rebounds to half or full 52‑week high. The asymmetry is clear: small downside risk versus large upside potential.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip after the first volume spike or positive drilling announcement. Size the position at 5–10% of capital, set a stop 10–15% below entry, and trail the stop as the price moves up.
playbook.if_watching: Trigger entry when the drilling results are positive and volume exceeds twice the average daily volume, or when the OTC listing is formally approved.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified; continue to monitor for the above catalysts. If a delisting notice or bankruptcy filing appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GWMO sits at a deep crash with a silent market; a positive drilling report or OTC listing could spark a 200% upside, making it a high‑risk, high‑reward contrarian play.
